REQ-1.name: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls

1.1.name: Network security control processes are defined and understood

1.2.name: Network security controls are configured and maintained

1.3.name: Network access to/from the CDE is restricted

1.4.name: Network connections between trusted and untrusted networks are controlled

1.5.name: Risks to the CDE from computing devices able to connect to untrusted networks

REQ-2.name: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components

2.1.name: Processes for secure configuration are defined and understood

2.2.name: System components are configured and managed securely

2.3.name: Wireless environments are configured and managed securely

3.1.name: Processes for protecting stored account data are defined

3.2.name: Storage of account data is kept to a minimum

3.3.name: Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not retained after authorization

3.4.name: Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy PAN is restricted

3.5.name: Primary account number (PAN) is secured wherever it is stored

3.6.name: Cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data are secured

3.7.name: Where cryptography is used to protect stored account data, key management processes are defined

REQ-4.name: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission

4.1.name: Processes for protecting cardholder data with cryptography are defined

4.2.name: PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission

REQ-5.name: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software

5.1.name: Processes to protect against malicious software are defined

5.2.name: Malicious software (malware) is prevented, or detected and addressed

5.3.name: Anti-malware mechanisms and processes are active, maintained, and monitored

5.4.name: Anti-phishing mechanisms protect users against phishing attacks

REQ-6.name: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software

6.1.name: Processes for security of bespoke and custom software are defined

6.2.name: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely

6.3.name: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed

6.4.name: Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks

6.5.name: Changes to all system components are managed securely

REQ-7.name: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know

7.1.name: Processes to restrict access are defined and understood

7.2.name: Access to system components and data is appropriately defined and assigned

7.3.name: Access to system components and data is managed via an access control system

REQ-8.name: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components

8.1.name: Processes for user identification and authentication are defined

8.2.name: User IDs and authentication credentials for all users are managed

8.3.name: User authentication before access to CDE is verified

8.5.name: Multi-factor authentication systems are configured securely

8.6.name: Use of application and system accounts and credentials is managed

REQ-9.name: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data

9.1.name: Processes to restrict physical access are defined and understood

9.2.name: Physical access controls manage entry into facilities and systems

9.3.name: Physical access for personnel and visitors is authorized and managed

9.4.name: Media with cardholder data is securely stored, accessed, distributed, and destroyed

9.5.name: Point of interaction (POI) devices are protected from tampering

REQ-10.name: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data

10.1.name: Processes for logging and monitoring are defined and understood

10.2.name: Audit logs capture all individual user access to cardholder data

10.3.name: Audit logs are protected from destruction and unauthorized modifications

10.4.name: Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies or suspicious activity

10.5.name: Audit log history is retained and available for analysis

10.6.name: Time-synchronization mechanisms support consistent time settings

10.7.name: Failures of critical security controls are detected and reported

REQ-11.name: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly

11.1.name: Processes for security testing are defined and understood

11.3.name: External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified

11.4.name: External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed

11.5.name: Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected

11.6.name: Unauthorized changes on payment pages are detected and reported

REQ-12.name: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

12.1.name: A comprehensive information security policy is established

12.3.name: Risks to the CDE are formally identified, evaluated, and managed

12.5.name: PCI DSS scope is documented and validated

12.7.name: Personnel are screened to reduce risks from insider threats

12.8.name: Risk to information assets associated with third-party service providers is managed

12.9.name: Third-party service providers support their customers' PCI DSS compliance

12.10.name: Suspected and confirmed security incidents are responded to immediately

CC-1.name: Change management policy and procedures are documented

CC-2.name: Changes are authorized prior to implementation

CC-3.name: Changes are tested in a separate environment prior to production

CC-4.name: Emergency changes follow an expedited but controlled process

CC-5.name: Change records are retained and reviewed periodically

LA-1.name: Access provisioning follows a formal request and approval process

LA-2.name: Privileged access is restricted and monitored

LA-3.name: User access is reviewed on a periodic basis

LA-4.name: Terminated user access is removed in a timely manner

LA-5.name: Password and authentication standards are enforced

LA-6.name: Segregation of duties is enforced for financially significant systems

OPS-1.name: Batch job processing is monitored and exceptions investigated

OPS-2.name: Incident management procedures are documented and followed

OPS-3.name: Backup and recovery procedures are documented and tested

OPS-4.name: System availability and performance are monitored

DR-1.name: A formal DR/BCP plan exists and is reviewed annually

DR-2.name: DR plan is tested at least annually

DR-3.name: RTO and RPO targets are defined and documented

DR-4.name: Critical system dependencies are identified in the DR plan
